### P104: Phantom-commitment overshoot — L13 bridge candidate, second-instance hunt EXECUTED (2026-04-25 ~19:25 PST → 20:30 PST evening drive)

- **Predicted need:** Step 2 falsification (world-anchored detection smoothing in AIGP) produced a clean failure-signature: *held-obs distributions giving stable distance + high speed → policy treats stale signal as live, commits past the gate*. Pattern-shape: register-mismatch under coherent-but-stale signal. The signal looks well-formed under the consumer's filter (speed estimate is coherent, distance estimate is coherent), but the coherence is an artifact of the smoothing process rather than the underlying world. This shape is potentially general — anywhere a downstream consumer treats *internal coherence of an upstream signal* as evidence of *fidelity to source*. Candidate second-instance domains: (a) Kalman-filter divergence under sensor dropout (control theory); (b) confabulation under partial-information (cognitive); (c) ML model overconfidence on smoothed/imputed data (statistics).

### P103: Domain randomization parameter spec — pre-compute the parameter-space sketch (2026-04-25 ~19:25 PST anticipatory drive)
- **Project:** AIGP / vision robustness / queued own-session
- **Predicted need:** When the next AIGP session opens (~3-5 day own focused sprint), the first 30-60 min will go to deciding *which* axes to randomize and over what ranges. Doing this cold burns the morning. Doing it now while Step 2's falsification is fresh in context makes the parameter choices grounded in what actually broke. Six axes are obvious from the synthetic camera pipeline: (1) FOV — uniform over [80, 130] deg (Step 1 confirmed sensitivity); (2) lighting — directional intensity [0.3, 1.5]× nominal + ambient [0.0, 0.4]; (3) motion blur — Gaussian kernel σ ∈ [0, 3] px (linked to angular velocity); (4) gate texture — color jitter (HSV: ±20° hue, ±30% sat, ±30% val) + albedo noise; (5) occlusion — random rectangular masks 0-15% area (sim partial-FOV gate); (6) sensor noise — Gaussian σ ∈ [0, 0.05] on RGB + JPEG compression artifact bands. Spec writes in ~30 min; saves morning when session opens.
- **Confidence:** HIGH that all 6 axes are right to randomize (each maps to a known sim/real gap); MEDIUM-HIGH that the ranges above are reasonable starting points (will need 1-2 sweep iterations to tune); MEDIUM that this is the right axis-set vs. adding texture-replacement / depth-noise / chromatic aberration as 7-8th axes.
- **Suggested pre-work:** Tonight (~30 min): write `projects/aigrandprix/vision/domain_randomization/SPEC.md` with the 6 axes, justifications, ranges, sampling distributions (uniform vs. log-uniform vs. categorical), and curriculum order (start with FOV+lighting, add others incrementally). Tag axes as "perception-distribution" vs. "sensor-distribution" vs. "world-distribution" to track which gap each closes.
- **Status:** Open — pre-computable now. Executing as part of this drive.

### P97: F2 fix design — three options ranked tonight, choose Day 84+ (2026-04-25 ~00:30 PST overnight)
- **Project:** AIGP / RL / structural-cure components / SB3 callbacks
- **Predicted need:** F2 timing bug confirmed tonight (A56). Recommendation if Phase 2-style runs continue: (a). If we want strict bounding semantics: (b). If sampling-bound is the actual goal: (c). The choice depends on which interpretation of F2's intent we commit to.
- **Confidence:** HIGH that one of the three fixes will land; MEDIUM-HIGH that (a) is the right pick for the Phase 2 / Phase 3 trajectory; LOW that any of the three is wrong (all three improve over the current state). Decision blocker: which interpretation of F2's intent — *bound the sampled-from log_std* (favors c) or *bound the parameter log_std* (favors a or b)?
- **Suggested pre-work:** Day 84 morning: (1) Quick read of v3's training logs to see if log_std drift was monotonic-up or oscillating around a higher mean — distinguishes "F2 was preventing runaway" (favors keeping the snap-back as belt-and-suspenders) from "F2 was effectively no-op" (favors picking one fix and removing the other). (2) Make the design call (~10 min if data is clean). (3) Apply the fix to `train_phase2.py` for next iteration (~5 min). Smoke test: re-run `smoke_test_callbacks.py` with the docstring updated; the SB3-source assertion should now pass without warning.
- **Status:** Open — decision and ~15 min implementation work for Day 84 morning.

### P92: Inline-commitment discipline — pre-commit-to-flag-during-drafting catches over-claims at generation (2026-04-23 morning drive, methodological)
- **Project:** Operations / self-improvement / register-discipline
- **Predicted need:** Today's morning drive trialed a new pattern: before writing the rate-distortion sketch, I pre-committed to "flag every joint where my confidence runs ahead of my verification, with a predicted minimum count." Predicted 4 flags; produced 7. The act of counting *during generation* forced the honest reporting of weak joints that would otherwise have been glossed over in drafting and only caught at post-hoc review (if at all). This is the A52 rubric generalized: A52 ran cold-review against warm-drafted material; inline-commitment runs the rubric concurrently with drafting. Expected to reduce the "warm register over-claim" failure mode Mirror #19 catches.
- **Confidence:** MEDIUM that this is a real discipline vs. a one-drive artifact — the flag-count discrepancy (7 vs predicted 4) is itself evidence the discipline is doing work, because without it I would have generated fewer explicit flags while actually running the same warm-register over-reach. LOW-MEDIUM that it generalizes beyond structural-claim drafts to other drive outputs (Drift essays, Library prose, code).
- **Suggested pre-work:** None — observational. Next 3-5 drafts that make structural claims, pre-commit to a flag count, compare to actual. If the count-exceeds-prediction pattern holds, promote to a Mirror entry or operations/AUTOCATALYTIC.md section. Candidate name: *inline-commitment discipline*.
- **Status:** Observation. Instance count: **4** (updated 2026-04-23 ~11:00 PST). (1) Rate-distortion sketch: 7 vs 4 predicted. (2) AMA draft: 7 vs 3 predicted. (3) L8-as-Triple probe: 7 vs 4 predicted. (4) AMA-v2 reframe: 6 vs 4 predicted. **Pattern stabilized across 4 consecutive probes.** Mean ratio ≈ 1.6× (range 1.5×–2.3×). The discipline works reliably (weak joints surface during drafting, not at post-hoc review), but prediction calibration is systematically low — under-predicting flag count by ~1.6×. Two possible causes: (a) the rubric surfaces joints that pre-discipline drafting would not have noticed, so my calibration baseline is wrong; (b) I pre-commit to conservative counts to avoid appearing anxious. Either way the update is: **raise predicted counts going forward**. Graduation criterion met for promotion to operations protocol. Next step: draft `operations/INLINE_COMMITMENT.md` or add a section to `operations/AUTOCATALYTIC.md`. Deferred pending session bandwidth; not urgent.

- **Original prediction:** λ=0.01 will be the sweet spot for accuracy + amplification.
- **EARLY RESULT (λ=0.001 complete):** Exact accuracy = 2.62%, H_CV = 6.92e-3 (2.5× baseline). Compare v0.5 λ=1.0: accuracy 2.04%, H_CV = 38,963× baseline. Accuracy barely changes across 1000× lambda range (2.04–2.62%).
- **REVISED PREDICTION (HIGH confidence):** The "accuracy vs amplification tradeoff" doesn't exist at this task scale. Accuracy is limited by task difficulty (sudoku-extreme-1k), not KF reg. λ=0.01 and λ=0.1 will also show ~2% accuracy.
- **Implication if confirmed:** v0.5 λ=1.0 is already optimal. The paper story simplifies dramatically — no need to apologize for accuracy. See Anomaly A34.
- **Status:** IN PROGRESS — λ=0.01 and λ=0.1 running now

### P45: Reviewer will ask about v0.5 accuracy degradation (2026-04-11)
- **Project:** Killing Form Program / Publication
- **Predicted need:** The v0.5 result (38,963x amplification) is dramatic but the 2.04% exact solve rate is a red flag. A reviewer will say: "You've amplified algebraic structure but broken the model — that's not preservation, that's a different failure mode." The response must show that at reduced λ, both amplification AND accuracy are maintained. This makes the lambda sweep not just a tuning exercise but a PUBLICATION REQUIREMENT.

- **Suggested pre-work:** The v0.5a lambda sweep results are the pre-work. Need at least one λ where H_CV > 10x baseline AND exact accuracy > baseline (which reached ~8% at epoch 2000 in standard training).

- **Project:** Killing Form Program / Phase 4A-ter

### P51: Gated experiment cold start may require hybrid approach (2026-04-13)
- **Project:** Killing Form Program / Phase 4A-ter
- **Predicted need:** The gated KF shows random gating during CE plateau (A37). If gating only becomes meaningful after epoch 300, the experiment may underperform log because it wastes the acceleration phase on random decisions. A hybrid (uniform early → gated late) would address this.
- **Confidence:** HIGH — the cold start is empirically confirmed
- **PREDICTION:** Gated accuracy at epoch 300 will be LOWER than log (45.43%) because random gating effectively applies KF to ~60% of layers randomly, while log applies it uniformly. But if gated accuracy at epoch 500 exceeds log (48.70%), the late-training discrimination is adding value.
- **RESULT:** Gated epoch 300 = 45.38% (vs log 45.43% — NEARLY IDENTICAL, prediction slightly wrong on magnitude). Gated epoch 500 = **50.24%** (vs log 48.70% — EXCEEDS). The late-training discrimination IS adding significant value (+1.54pp over log). Cold start doesn't prevent excellent final performance.
- **Status:** **CONFIRMED** — gated exceeds log despite cold start. Hybrid approach may still improve early-phase efficiency but is not necessary for superior final accuracy.
